mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates for equity valuations, directly increasing present values of future corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0%, it signals weakening labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, which compresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or out-of-sample hit rate falls below 55% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate robust aggregate demand across durable and non-durable sectors, which expands corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month acceleration in the industrial production index reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply-side recovery, which boosts short-term equity momentum and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of instances following an INDPRO acceleration, or the signal's predictive power vanishes outside the post-shock recovery regime (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 300.0, it indicates entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing borrowing costs and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of triggered observations, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for concurrent Fed funds rate changes in a panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate signal a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative 60-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing over a 4-year horizon.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarter-over-quarter increase in real GDP reflects underlying economic growth that translates into higher corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as markets price in improved fundamentals.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of instances following positive GDP growth, or the Rank IC for the GDP signal remains below 0.03 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 100.0, it indicates a contraction in manufacturing and industrial output that signals economic weakness, reducing corporate profit expectations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the threshold condition fails to improve predictive accuracy over a baseline model in walk-forward validation.
